session_hint: the S3 presigned URL 403 error investigation
question: What did we rule out when investigating the S3 presigned URL 403 errors?
turns: About 15% of S3 presigned GET URLs return 403 Access Denied for end users. Direct SDK GetObject calls from the backend work fine. First suspect: the IAM role used to sign the URLs lacks s3:GetObject permission on the bucket.

Verified the IAM role policy: it has s3:GetObject on arn:aws:s3:::media-uploads/* with no explicit denies anywhere in the chain. Tested the role directly with the AWS CLI — works fine. IAM permissions are correct, ruling them out.

Checked the bucket policy for IP-based restrictions or VPC endpoint conditions that might block external clients downloading via presigned URLs. The bucket policy only enforces HTTPS — no IP conditions, no VPC endpoint requirement. Bucket policy is not causing the 403s.

Found it: the signing service runs on EC2 instances with misconfigured NTP. System clocks drift up to 6 minutes. Presigned URLs have a 5-minute expiry, so URLs signed near the end of a drift window are already expired by the time users click them. Fixed NTP with chronyc and the 403 rate dropped to zero.
